_comment: Configuration for 68-user-mgmt: cross-OS user/group creation. Linux uses useradd/groupadd, macOS uses dscl. CODE RED: every file/path error must include exact path + reason.

password._comment: Plain-text passwords accepted via CLI/JSON to mirror the Windows side. Always prefer --password-file (chmod 600) for non-throwaway accounts. Logs NEVER record passwords.

json._comment: JSON inputs may be a single object or an array. Both shapes auto-detected at parse time.

manifestRetention._comment: Auto-cleanup policy for ssh-key rollback manifests under /var/lib/68-user-mgmt/ssh-key-runs. Applied (a) by remove-ssh-keys.sh --prune, and (b) opportunistically after a successful add-user.sh run that wrote a manifest, so the dir self-maintains. Disable per-run with --no-auto-prune (or UM_NO_AUTO_PRUNE=1). Each policy is OR-combined: a manifest is removed if it matches ANY enabled policy. Set a value to 0 to disable that specific policy.

summaryDiscovery._comment: Glob patterns used by verify-summary.sh to auto-discover summary JSON files under the runs directory. Patterns are evaluated relative to --root (default: <UM_MANIFEST_DIR>/, falling back to /var/lib/68-user-mgmt/ssh-key-runs). Use --recursive (or set recursiveDefault=true) to enable bash globstar (**). Override per-run with --glob PATTERN (repeatable) -- when supplied on CLI, these defaults are ignored. Each pattern is OR-combined; matched files are de-duplicated by absolute path before validation. CODE RED: missing root or unreadable matches always log the exact path + reason.
